mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and rising consumer price inflation indicates an overheating economy, prompting anticipated monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high-GDP-high-inflation periods yield positive 60d returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero signals immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of negative industrial production months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real final sales to domestic purchasers reflect strengthening domestic demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in less than 45% of periods with this labor market divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% creates a negative real rate environment that eventually forces aggressive central bank tightening, depressing equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals strong construction sector demand and wealth effects, lifting equity markets over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances during expansion phases.
